name: @vibe-engineer-starter/golden-client-fixture
description: I-16B golden-client fixture: the GENERATED/SHARED client surface for the starter golden-records boundary — derived ONLY from the I-16A canonical contract (re-export/wrap of the I-16A generated client; no Zod schema / route-contract re-declaration / hand-authored fetch DTO), a framework-neutral consumer hook consumed by BOTH web and mobile, and per-platform transport adapters (web DOM-fetch-shape + mobile RN-fetch-shape) that feed the SAME shared client. Domain-neutral sample/demo/reference (DL-14 / DL-16 / DL-20A). Mirrors the proven I-16A pattern.

vibeEngineer.consumptionModel: consumes I-16A contract-derived client/types only (no duplicated contract source)
vibeEngineer.boundaryRule: shared client derives ONLY from the I-16A canonical contract (DL-14 §5); per-platform transports differ only in fetch/baseURL/auth (DL-14 §60) and must NOT fork the route contract / schemas

//dependency-model: Graph-neutral: this fixture path is NOT a pnpm workspace member (pnpm-workspace.yaml = packages/*, packages/presets/*, packages/adapters/*, infra/pulumi), so the modeled runtime deps below add ZERO edges to the real harness workspace graph and require no lockfile reconciliation. The REAL resolve+runtime of @ts-rest/core + zod is exercised by the golden-flow witness runner via an ESM resolve loader hook (golden-flow/src/runtime/dep-resolver-hooks.mjs) anchored at the harness packages/contracts context (the validated I-15B-3 in-context resolution precedent) — NO install, NO manifest/lockfile edit, NO mock.
